response:
Emily collected 24 blueberries which became the starting point for the group’s gathering. David collected 15 more than Emily which meant he had 39 blueberries in total. This number was also three times what Sophia had collected. So Sophia had 13 blueberries which when added to Emily and David’s totals gave a combined count of 76 blueberries.

***

They used these blueberries to make muffins and each batch needed 9 blueberries. With 76 blueberries available they could make several batches. Dividing 76 by 9 showed that they could prepare 8 full batches with 4 blueberries left over. These leftover blueberries were not enough for another batch so they were not used.

***

Each batch of muffins was sold for 5 dollars. With 8 batches made the total amount of money they earned was 40 dollars. This amount came directly from the number of batches they could prepare using the blueberries they collected. The leftover blueberries did not contribute to the earnings.

***

The process involved gathering blueberries preparing muffins and selling them. All steps were connected and depended on the initial collection. The final amount they made was 40 dollars and the journey reflected the efforts of youth.
